title: rubus × aureliacensis
intro: Rubus × aureliacensis, also known as the aurelian raspberry, is a hybrid species of raspberry (Rubus idaeus) native to Europe, resulting from a cross between the wild raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). The medicinal properties of Rubus × aureliacensis include its high content of antioxidants and tannins, which contribute to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × aureliacensis include improved cardiovascular health, enhanced cognitive function, and a reduced risk of certain types of cancer due to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid. Rubus × aureliacensis can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
